title: rosa communis var. obtusa
intro: Rosa communis var. obtusa, also known as the Dwarf Wild Rose, is a variety of wild rose that is native to Europe and Asia. This shrub has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Rosa communis var. obtusa include its ability to reduce swelling, promote wound healing, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
